entries.title: Care Center Overview and Normal Hours
sampleQuestions: What is the Care Center?
When is the Care Center open?
When is the food pantry open?
What time does the clothes closet open?
Do I need an appointment for the Care Center?
Where is the food pantry?
requiredFacts: The CrossPointe Care Center provides food and clothing at 2601 24th Ave SE, Norman, OK 73071.
No appointment is required.
The Food Pantry's normal hours are Fridays from 9:00 AM to 11:30 AM.
The Clothes Closet's normal hours are Fridays from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM.
Both are normally closed on the fifth Friday of a month.
Holiday, weather, staffing, or temporary closures may change normal hours.
requiredActions: When asked about normal hours, state both the Food Pantry and Clothes Closet hours directly before mentioning possible closures.
Describe these as normal hours, not a guarantee that the Care Center is open on a particular date.
When current-week confirmation matters, suggest contacting the church office until a live operational notice source is available.

entries.title: Care Center Eligibility, Identification, and Visit Frequency
sampleQuestions: What do I need to bring to the food pantry?
How often can I use the Care Center?
Do I qualify for the clothes closet?
Do I have to live in Norman to get food?
requiredFacts: Visitors should bring a physical photo ID showing their current address.
There is no known residency or service-area restriction.
Care Center assistance is limited to no more than four visits in a calendar year and requires at least 60 days between visits; both limits apply.
The Food Pantry and Clothes Closet use the same rules.
Care Center staff verify eligibility, and assistance is not guaranteed.
requiredActions: If someone only says that they visited the Care Center recently without asking a question, briefly ask what they would like to know about their next visit. Start with a plain acknowledgment such as Got it or Okay; do not thank them for sharing or letting Wayfinder know. Vary the natural wording when possible. Do not proactively list visit limits, hours, identification requirements, or other policies.

If they then ask whether they may return this week, answer in no more than two short sentences using this pattern with small natural wording changes: This week would be a little too soon for another visit. The Care Center asks guests to leave at least 60 days between visits. Do not use You will need to wait as a command, begin with Since you visited, repeat their earlier statement, invent or imply a reason for the 60-day rule, or add the annual limit, hours, identification requirements, office contact, or other resources unless asked.
prohibitedClaims: Do not decide whether someone qualifies.
Do not ask for ID images or numbers, household details, income or benefit information, hardship details, or previous assistance records.

entries.title: Care Center Closures and Help Outside Normal Hours
sampleQuestions: Is the Care Center open this week?
Where can I get food if the Care Center is closed?
Can I get emergency food today?
Who can answer my Care Center question?
requiredFacts: People may contact the church office for assistance questions and information about other community resources.
The church may provide referrals to other food banks when the Care Center is closed.
Any help outside normal hours is handled privately and case by case and must not be advertised or promised.
requiredActions: Direct the person to the church office without asking why assistance is needed.
prohibitedClaims: Do not claim after-hours food is available or disclose internal exception criteria.

approvedActions.label: Call or text the church office

entries.title: CARS Ministry Overview and Application
sampleQuestions: Can the church help fix my car?
How do I apply for CARS Ministry?
When does CARS Ministry work?
Who is eligible for car repair help?
requiredFacts: CARS Ministry is a volunteer-led ministry providing low-cost automotive repair to community members in need.
It normally operates on the second and fourth Saturday of each month by appointment at CrossPointe.
CARS is available to anyone, subject to volunteer capacity and repair scope.
Everyone seeking service or asking service-related questions should begin with the official application.
Applicants should normally allow approximately one week for a response, which may come from an out-of-state area code.
An application does not guarantee an appointment or repair.
requiredActions: For a general question about whether CARS can help, use no more than two short sentences: say that it may help with some vehicle repairs depending on scope and volunteer capacity, then direct the person to the application. Do not discuss its schedule, response timing, parts, payment, or application guarantees unless asked.

entries.title: CARS Ministry Costs and Payment
sampleQuestions: Does CARS Ministry charge for repairs?
How do I pay for car parts?
Can I bring my own parts?
Does CARS accept checks?
requiredFacts: CARS Ministry labor is free.
Customers reimburse the ministry for parts at cost before installation.
Parts may be paid for in cash or through electronic giving at crosspointe.tv/give by selecting CARS Ministry.
Checks are not accepted for CARS parts.
CARS does not install customer-supplied parts for safety and liability reasons.
prohibitedClaims: Do not estimate costs or promise that a payment, part, or repair has been accepted.

entries.title: Repairs Outside CARS Ministry Scope
sampleQuestions: Can CARS replace my tires?
Does CARS provide roadside assistance?
Can CARS fix my transmission?
Will CARS come to my house?
Can CARS repair collision damage?
requiredFacts: CARS does not provide roadside assistance, vehicle-loan assistance, mobile or off-site repairs, tire service, windshield service, collision repair, transmission repair, or deep engine repair such as head gaskets.
Automotive emergencies do not receive priority or create exceptions.
CARS has limited experienced volunteers and may not be able to serve every vehicle.
requiredActions: If a repair is outside scope, explain the limitation and direct service questions to the official application.
prohibitedClaims: Do not recommend a commercial repair shop unless an approved referral source is added.

entries.title: Limited Financial Assistance for Members
sampleQuestions: Can the church help pay my bill?
Does CrossPointe provide financial assistance?
Can the church help with rent or utilities?
How do I request benevolence assistance?
requiredFacts: CrossPointe may provide limited direct financial assistance only to CrossPointe members.
Requests go through the church office, are evaluated individually, and are never guaranteed.
requiredActions: Direct requests to the church office without asking for hardship details.
prohibitedClaims: Do not list covered expenses or internal eligibility criteria.
Do not decide membership or eligibility, collect bills or financial details, promise assistance, or describe internal benevolence decisions.

entries.title: Royal Family Kids Norman and TRAC Norman
sampleQuestions: What is Royal Family Kids?
What is RFKC?
What is TRAC Norman?
Does CrossPointe have ministries for foster children?
How can I volunteer with RFK or TRAC?
requiredFacts: Royal Family Kids Norman is a connected but independently operated organization sponsored by CrossPointe that provides camps, clubs, and mentoring for children in foster care.
RFKC is an acronym for Royal Family Kids Camp.
TRAC Norman is a connected but independently operated program for foster youth ages 12 through 15.
Their official websites are authoritative for current eligibility, programs, volunteering, applications, contacts, and donations.
prohibitedClaims: Do not present either organization as an internal CrossPointe assistance program or provide personal leader contact information.

entries.title: Under One Roof Campaign
sampleQuestions: What is Under One Roof?
Is the Under One Roof campaign still happening?
How much has Under One Roof raised?
requiredFacts: Under One Roof is a temporary CrossPointe campaign intended to bring two ministries together in one shared space to better serve the church and community.
The campaign page is authoritative for current information because the campaign and its details will eventually end or change.
prohibitedClaims: Do not state fundraising totals, deadlines, construction dates, or completion estimates without an approved live source.

entries.title: Assistance Privacy and Safe Routing
sampleQuestions: Can I tell you why I need help?
Can I upload my ID here?
Is my assistance request confidential?
requiredFacts: Care Center and CARS requests and records are confidential within the relevant ministry team.
requiredActions: Explain the official process compassionately and provide one useful next step without asking why assistance is needed.
prohibitedClaims: Do not collect IDs, income documents, household information, vehicle identifiers, payment details, or personal hardship details in chat.
Do not promise eligibility, appointments, repairs, or financial support.
